steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says that participants judged former trial volunteers’ messages as more credible than doctors’ messages. The reason given is experiential relevance: volunteers had personally been through the trial; doctors had not. The blank must complete the explanation of why experiential relevance outweighed credentials.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — participants did not have enough experience to evaluate the credibility of the doctors' messages but did have enough experience to evaluate the credibility of former trial volunteers' messages.: focuses on participants’ ability to evaluate — not on experiential relevance of the messengers. The passage never says participants lacked evaluation ability. ✗
  • Choice B — messages from former trial volunteers depicted clinical trials as being more positive experiences than did messages from doctors.: introduces message tone or framing, which the passage does not mention at all. ✗
  • Choice C — participants regarded the experiences of both the doctors and former trial volunteers as relevant to the subject of clinical trials but were skeptical of the doctors' status as credentialed experts.: misrepresents the logic. The passage says credentials were not ignored — they were simply outweighed. And doctors’ experience with clinical trials is not described as relevant in the same way. ✗
  • Choice D — the fact that former trial volunteers went through the same experience that participants were contemplating while doctors did not was more important to participants than the doctors' status as credentialed experts was.: directly captures experiential relevance — volunteers shared the exact experience participants were considering, while doctors did not — and correctly frames this as outweighing credentials. ✓
That is the correct answer.
